name: Cucumber (food medium)
basis: per 100 g fresh weight (raw, with peel)

components.name: water
components.evidence: Cucumber with peel raw is ~95% water; matrix is essentially a dilute aqueous solution.

components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence: mM assumes 100 g fresh ~= 100 mL aqueous (95% water); MW 180.16.

components.name: D-fructose
components.evidence: Fructose slightly exceeds glucose in cucumber flesh; dominant fermentable sugars. MW 180.16.

components.name: sucrose
components.evidence: Minor; cucumber flesh is predominantly reducing sugars. MW 342.30.

components.name: maltose
components.evidence: Trace. MW 342.30.

components.name: starch
components.evidence: Storage/structural glucan; slowly fermentable.

components.name: dietary fiber
components.evidence: Cell-wall polysaccharides (cellulose/pectin).

components.name: malic acid
components.source: Organic-acid survey of cucumber ~2024 mg/kg FW (PubMed 3811599; McFeeters 1982 doi:10.1111/j.1365-2621.1982.tb12899.x); TWU sugar/acid quantification study
components.evidence: Malic acid is the dominant organic acid in immature (edible-stage) cucumber; was MISSING from the FooDB record. MW 134.09; mM per 100 mL basis.

components.name: citric acid
components.source: Organic-acid survey ~801 mg/kg FW (PubMed 3811599; McFeeters 1982 doi:10.1111/j.1365-2621.1982.tb12899.x)
components.evidence: Second organic acid; rises to become dominant only in over-mature fruit. Was MISSING from FooDB record. MW 192.12.

components.name: fumaric acid
components.evidence: Reported present as a minor organic acid in cucumber; not reliably quantified. Was missing from FooDB.

components.name: total protein
components.evidence: Low-protein matrix; amino acids below are the protein-derived profile unless flagged 'free'.

components.name: L-glutamine (free)
components.source: ACS Food Sci Technol doi:10.1021/acsfoodscitech.6c00012 (flesh range 149.5-333.2 mg/100g; midpoint used); Moore 2022 doi:10.1111/1750-3841.15990
components.evidence: Predominant FREE amino acid in cucumber flesh; growth-critical N source. Was absent from FooDB (only Levoglutamide=glutamine appears in uncovered).

components.name: L-citrulline (free)
components.source: ACS doi:10.1021/acsfoodscitech.6c00012 (flesh 6.5-51.5 mg/100g); MDPI Horticulturae 5:22 (2019); Kenya market survey EJFood 2020
components.evidence: Cucurbit-characteristic free amino acid; midpoint of flesh range. Higher in peel.

components.name: gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA, free)
components.evidence: Reported among the notable free amino acids of fresh cucumber; not quantified here.

components.name: L-asparagine (free)
components.evidence: Among predominant free amino acids; unquantified.

components.name: L-glutamate
components.evidence: Most abundant protein-bound amino acid.

components.name: L-arginine
components.evidence: Also a major free amino acid (ACS study).

components.name: L-serine
components.evidence: Among predominant free amino acids.

components.name: biotin (B7)
components.evidence: Present but not quantified in USDA; trace.

components.name: vitamin A (as carotenoids, RAE)
components.evidence: No preformed retinol; provitamin-A carotenoids (see carotenes below).

components.name: phylloquinone (vitamin K1)
components.evidence: Cucumber's most notable micronutrient (~14% DV); concentrated in peel.

components.name: potassium
components.evidence: Dominant mineral.

components.name: boron
components.evidence: Genuine plant micronutrient; kept as trace mineral though unquantified.

components.name: molybdate
components.evidence: Trace essential micronutrient.

components.name: iodide
components.evidence: Trace.

components.name: chloride
components.evidence: Counter-ion; present.

components.name: sulfate
components.evidence: Sulfur source.

components.name: nitrate/ammonium (inorganic N)
components.evidence: Cucumber accumulates nitrate; inorganic N pool for microbial growth.

components.name: total fat
components.evidence: Very low-fat matrix.

components.name: linoleic acid (18:2 n-6)
components.evidence: Principal PUFA.

components.name: palmitic acid (16:0)
components.evidence: Principal SFA.

remove.name: Silver
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace contaminant, not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Arsenate
remove.reason: toxic metalloid contaminant, not a nutrient

remove.name: Cadmium
remove.reason: toxic heavy-metal contaminant

remove.name: Lead
remove.reason: toxic heavy-metal contaminant

remove.name: Mercury
remove.reason: toxic heavy-metal contaminant

remove.name: Nickel
remove.reason: non-essential trace contaminant in this matrix

remove.name: Chromium
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace element, not a growth nutrient here

remove.name: Co2+
remove.reason: cobalt only biologically relevant via B12, which cucumber lacks; FooDB trace noise

remove.name: Aluminum
remove.reason: non-nutritive contaminant

remove.name: Iridium
remove.reason: non-nutritive contaminant, biologically implausible

remove.name: Strontium
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace contaminant

remove.name: Barium
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace contaminant

remove.name: Titanium
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace contaminant

remove.name: Rubidium
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace element

remove.name: Zirconium
remove.reason: non-nutritive contaminant

remove.name: Lithium
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace element

remove.name: Tin
remove.reason: non-nutritive contaminant

remove.name: Tungsten
remove.reason: non-nutritive contaminant

remove.name: Tellurium
remove.reason: non-nutritive contaminant

remove.name: Vanadium
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace element

remove.name: Silicon
remove.reason: non-nutritive structural trace, not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Creatine cytosol
remove.reason: animal metabolite, biologically implausible in a plant; FooDB predicted

remove.name: Taurine C2H7NO3S
remove.reason: animal metabolite, implausible in cucumber; FooDB predicted

remove.name: L-Methionine Sulfoxide
remove.reason: oxidation artifact of methionine, not a distinct nutrient

remove.name: NG,NG-Dimethyl-L-arginine
remove.reason: ADMA is an animal metabolite; implausible/predicted here

remove.name: (R)-Oxypeucedanin
remove.reason: furanocoumarin characteristic of Apiaceae, not cucumber; FooDB predicted

remove.name: epsilon-Polylysine
remove.reason: industrial microbial preservative, not an endogenous cucumber compound

remove.name: Histamine
remove.reason: biogenic amine not characteristic of fresh cucumber; FooDB predicted

remove.name: 24epsilon-Ethyl-31-norlanosta-8,25(27)-dien-3beta-ol
remove.reason: predicted odd phytosterol, not measured; noise

remove.name: D-Ornithine
remove.reason: D-enantiomer implausible; L-ornithine trace at most; predicted noise

remove.name: D-Serine
remove.reason: D-enantiomer implausible in plant free-AA pool; predicted noise

missing_added: malic acid (dominant organic acid, ~0.2 g/100g) - absent from FooDB record
citric acid (~0.08 g/100g) - absent from FooDB record
quantitative individual sugars (glucose 0.77, fructose 0.87, sucrose 0.04 g/100g)
free L-glutamine (~0.24 g/100g) and free L-citrulline - dominant free amino acids
GABA and asparagine as free amino acids
quantitative vitamin/mineral panel (K 147 mg, vitamin K1 16.3 ug, etc.)
keep_uncovered: cucurbitacins (bitter triterpenoids, defining cucumber phytochemical - genuine even if not in current list)
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 168409 (Cucumber, with peel, raw)
ACS Food Sci. Technol. 2023 doi:10.1021/acsfoodscitech.6c00012 (free amino acids, organic acids, sugars in 8 cucumber varieties, flesh vs peel)
McFeeters et al. 1982 J. Food Sci. doi:10.1111/j.1365-2621.1982.tb12899.x (malic & citric acids in pickling cucumbers)
Organic acids of vegetables IV, PubMed 3811599
Moore et al. 2022 J. Food Sci. doi:10.1111/1750-3841.15990 (free amino acid profile of pickling cucumber)
TWU quantification of sugars and organic acids in 210 cucumber accessions
notes: Cucumber is a ~95% water, low-solute matrix. Dominant carbon/energy sources for a microbial medium are the free hexoses fructose (~0.87) and glucose (~0.77 g/100g) plus malic acid (~0.2 g/100g), the latter a key organic acid that was entirely missing from the FooDB record. Nitrogen is supplied largely by the free amino acids glutamine, citrulline, arginine and serine. Removed 30+ FooDB noise entries (toxic/trace heavy metals and predicted animal/non-cucumber metabolites).
